result.markdown:
--- title: "India's Fertilizer Import Landscape: Supply Concentration & Price Dynamics" description: "A detailed analysis of India's fertilizer imports — Urea, DAP, Rock Phosphate, Phosphoric Acid, and MOP — covering source-country concentration, price volatility, and domestic production trends." author: "Pawan" date: "2025/11/26" date-modified: last-modified categories: - "Trade" - "Fertilizer" - "Agriculture" format: html: code-fold: true toc: true toc-depth: 3 theme: cosmo editor_options: chunk_output_type: console execute: error: false message: false warning: false eval: true --- ## Overview India is the world's second-largest consumer of fertilizers. With domestic production insufficient to meet demand for nitrogenous (Urea), phosphatic (DAP, Rock Phosphate, Phosphoric Acid), and potassic (MOP) nutrients, India imports substantial quantities each year. This analysis examines FY 2016–2024 import trends, source-country concentration, international price volatility, and domestic Urea/DAP production. --- ## Setup ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(data.table) library(ggplot2) library(ggiraph) library(scales) library(gt) library(viridis) library(plotly) library(tidyr) library(patchwork) options(scipen = 99999) my_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 13) + theme( plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 8)), plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.5, color = "#7F8C8D", margin = margin(b = 10)), plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, color = "#95A5A6", hjust = 0), axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"), axis.text = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1), legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank(), panel.grid.major= element_line(color = "#ECF0F1", linewidth = 0.4), panel.grid.minor= element_blank(), strip.background= element_rect(fill = "#F8F9FA", color = NA), strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11) ) ## Interactive stacked bar helper make_fert_girafe <- function(data, title, subtitle, pal = NULL) { data <- as.data.table(data) data[, tt := paste0("", Country, "
Year: ", Year, "
Qty: ", round(Qty,2), " MT
Share: ", Percent, "%")] p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = Year, y = Qty, fill = Country)) + geom_bar_interactive(aes(tooltip = tt, data_id = Country), stat = "identity", position = "stack", width = 0.75, alpha = 0.9) + geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Percent < 9, "", paste0(Percent, "%"))), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 3, color = "white", fontface = "bold") + my_theme + scale_y_continuous(labels = comma_format()) + labs(x = NULL, y = "Qty (Million Tonnes)", title = title, subtitle = subtitle, caption = "Source: Ministry of Commerce, India | Analysis: Pawan") if (!is.null(pal)) p <- p + scale_fill_manual(values = pal) else p <- p + scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") girafe(ggobj = p, width_svg = 11, height_svg = 6, options = list( opts_tooltip(css = "background:#fff;color:#333;padding:8px;border-radius:5px;border:1px solid #ddd;font-size:12px;"), opts_hover(css = "opacity:0.85;stroke:#333;stroke-width:1.5;"), opts_hover_inv(css = "opacity:0.3;"), opts_zoom(min = 0.8, max = 3), opts_sizing(rescale = TRUE, width = 1))) } ## Data loading & cleaning helper load_fert <- function(path) { dt <- readRDS(path) dt$ImportMonth <- gsub(",","",dt$ImportMonth) dt } prep_country <- function(dt, keep_countries, factor_levels) { dt <- dt[Country != "Total", .(Qty = sum(as.numeric(ImportMonth), na.rm=TRUE)), .(Country, Year)] dt[!(Country %in% keep_countries), Country := "OTHER COUNTRY"] dt <- dt[, .(Qty = sum(Qty)), .(Country, Year)] dt[, Qty := Qty/100000] dt <- dt[Year > "2015", .(Qty = sum(Qty)), .(Country, Year)] dt[, Percent := round(Qty*100/sum(Qty)), by = Year] dt[, Country := factor(Country, levels = factor_levels)] dt } ``` ::: --- ## Section 1 · Urea Imports Urea is India's most consumed fertilizer. Despite domestic production, India imports 6–10 million tonnes annually. China, Iran, and Oman dominate supply; Russia has emerged as a key source in recent years. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} raw_urea <- load_fert("/home/pawan/fertilizer/urea_importqty.rds") raw_urea[Country == "CHINA P RP", Country := "CHINA"] raw_urea[Country == "EGYPT A RP", Country := "EGYPT"] t_urea <- prep_country( raw_urea, keep_countries = c("CHINA","OMAN","IRAN","UKRAINE","EGYPT","RUSSIA","INDONESIA"), factor_levels = c("OTHER COUNTRY","INDONESIA","EGYPT","RUSSIA","UKRAINE","OMAN","IRAN","CHINA") ) make_fert_girafe(t_urea, title = "India's Urea Imports by Source Country", subtitle = "China, Iran & Oman historically dominant; Russia emerging") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: --- ## Section 2 · DAP Imports Di-ammonium phosphate (DAP) is the primary phosphatic fertilizer in India. China and Saudi Arabia have historically led supply; Morocco and Russia play secondary roles. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} raw_dap <- load_fert("/home/pawan/fertilizer/dap_importqty.rds") raw_dap[Country == "CHINA P RP", Country := "CHINA"] t_dap <- prep_country( raw_dap, keep_countries = c("CHINA","SAUDI ARAB","MOROCCO","JORDAN","RUSSIA"), factor_levels = c("OTHER COUNTRY","MOROCCO","JORDAN","RUSSIA","CHINA","SAUDI ARAB") ) make_fert_girafe(t_dap, title = "India's DAP Imports by Source Country", subtitle = "Saudi Arabia & China lead; Morocco & Russia provide diversification") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: --- ## Section 3 · Rock Phosphate Imports Rock phosphate is the upstream input for phosphoric acid and superphosphates. Jordan and Morocco — both in the phosphate-rich North Africa/Middle East belt — dominate India's supply. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} raw_rp <- load_fert("/home/pawan/fertilizer/rock_phosphate_importqty.rds") raw_rp[Country == "EGYPT A RP", Country := "EGYPT"] t_rp <- prep_country( raw_rp, keep_countries = c("EGYPT","JORDAN","MOROCCO","PERU","TOGO"), factor_levels = c("OTHER COUNTRY","PERU","TOGO","EGYPT","MOROCCO","JORDAN") ) make_fert_girafe(t_rp, title = "India's Rock Phosphate Imports by Source Country", subtitle = "Jordan & Morocco dominate; geographic concentration is a risk") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: --- ## Section 4 · Phosphoric Acid Imports ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} raw_pa <- load_fert("/home/pawan/fertilizer/phosphoric_acid_importqty.rds") t_pa <- prep_country( raw_pa, keep_countries = c("MOROCCO","JORDAN","SENEGAL"), factor_levels = c("OTHER COUNTRY","SENEGAL","JORDAN","MOROCCO") ) make_fert_girafe(t_pa, title = "India's Phosphoric Acid Imports by Source Country", subtitle = "Morocco leads; Senegal and Jordan secondary suppliers") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: --- ## Section 5 · MOP (Muriate of Potash) Imports India has no domestic potash resources and imports 100% of its MOP needs. Belarus and Canada have historically led, with Russia and Jordan filling gaps. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} raw_mop <- load_fert("/home/pawan/fertilizer/potash_importqty.rds") t_mop <- prep_country( raw_mop, keep_countries = c("CANADA","BELARUS","ISRAEL","JORDAN","LITHUANIA","RUSSIA"), factor_levels = c("OTHER COUNTRY","RUSSIA","LITHUANIA","JORDAN","ISRAEL","BELARUS","CANADA") ) make_fert_girafe(t_mop, title = "India's MOP Imports by Source Country", subtitle = "Canada & Belarus historically dominant; Russia share rising post-2022") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: --- ## Section 6 · International Fertilizer Prices Global fertilizer prices are a major driver of India's import bill and subsidy expenditure. The 2021–22 price spike — driven by energy costs, sanctions, and supply disruptions — dramatically increased India's fertilizer subsidy burden. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} price <- fread("/home/pawan/fertilizer/international_fert_price.csv") price[, Year := paste0("20", Year)] price[, Month := substr(Month, 1, 3)] price[, Date := as.Date(paste(Year, Month, 20, sep="-"), format="%Y-%b-%d")] price_long <- melt(price, id.vars = c("Month","Year","Date")) price_long <- price_long[variable %in% c("Urea","DAP","MOP")] ## Plotly line chart — richer interactivity p_price <- ggplot(price_long, aes(x=Date, y=value, color=variable, text=paste0(variable,"\n",format(Date,"%b %Y"), "\n$",round(value)," /MT"))) + geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) + geom_smooth(method="loess", se=FALSE, linewidth=0.6, linetype="dashed", alpha=0.5) + facet_wrap(~variable, scales="free_y") + scale_color_brewer(palette="Set1") + my_theme + theme(legend.position="none") + scale_x_date(date_labels="%b '%y", date_breaks="6 months") + labs(title="International Fertilizer Prices (USD/MT)", subtitle="2021–22 price shock visible in all three fertilizers", y="Price (USD/MT)", x=NULL, caption="Source: World Bank / FAO | Analysis: Pawan") ggplotly(p_price, tooltip="text") |> layout(hovermode="x unified") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Year-on-Year Price Change ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} price_annual <- price_long[, .(Avg_Price = mean(value, na.rm=TRUE)), .(Year, variable)] setorder(price_annual, variable, Year) price_annual[, YoY := round((Avg_Price/shift(Avg_Price)-1)*100, 1), by=variable] gt(dcast(price_annual, Year ~ variable, value.var="Avg_Price")) |> tab_header(title="Annual Average International Fertilizer Price (USD/MT)") |> fmt_number(columns=2:4, decimals=0) |> data_color(columns=c("Urea","DAP","MOP"), colors=scales::col_numeric(c("#D5F5E3","#FDFEFE","#FADBD8"), domain=NULL)) |> opt_stylize(style=3) |> tab_source_note("Source: World Bank Commodity Prices") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
Annual Average International Fertilizer Price (USD/MT)
Year Urea DAP MOP
2017 228 365 233
2018 281 422 264
2019 272 359 288
2020 263 331 248
2021 524 618 284
2022 656 861 572
2023 380 570 434
2024 347 590 301
2025 383 633 283
Source: World Bank Commodity Prices
``` ::: ::: --- ## Section 7 · Domestic Urea & DAP Production ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} fert <- readRDS("/home/pawan/fertilizer/production-import/fert_production_import.RDS") fert <- fert[Year != "Total"] fert[, Date := as.Date(paste(Year,"-",Month,"-",15,sep=""), "%Y-%b-%d")] fert_long <- melt(fert, id.vars=c("Year","Month","Date")) fert_long <- fert_long[variable!="SNo" & variable!="Blank"] fert_long <- separate(fert_long, variable, into=c("Fertilizer","Variable"), sep="_") setDT(fert_long) prod_old <- fert_long[Variable=="Production"] prod_old[, value := as.numeric(value)/100000] prod_old[, Year := as.integer(Year)] fert1 <- fread("/home/pawan/fertilizer/production-import.csv") fert1[, Date := as.Date(paste(Year,"-",Month,"-",15,sep=""), "%Y-%b-%d")] fert1_long <- melt(fert1, id.vars=c("Year","Month","Date","Fertilizer")) fert1_long[variable == "Production_Achievement", variable := "Production"] prod_new <- fert1_long[variable == "Production"] prod_new[, Year := as.integer(Year)] prod_all <- rbind( prod_old[Year < 2018, .(Year,Month,Date,Fertilizer,value)], prod_new[, .(Year,Month,Date,Fertilizer,value)] ) prod_all <- prod_all[Fertilizer %in% c("Urea","DAP") & !is.na(value) & Year != 2025] prod_annual <- prod_all[, .(Qty = sum(value, na.rm=TRUE)), .(Year, Fertilizer)] ## Area + line combo p_prod <- ggplot(prod_annual, aes(x=Year, y=Qty, color=Fertilizer, fill=Fertilizer, text=paste0(Fertilizer," (",Year,"): ",round(Qty,2)," MT"))) + geom_area(alpha=0.2, position="identity") + geom_line(linewidth=1.3) + geom_point(size=2.5) + facet_wrap(~Fertilizer, scales="free_y", ncol=1) + scale_color_manual(values=c("Urea"="#3498DB","DAP"="#E67E22")) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("Urea"="#3498DB","DAP"="#E67E22")) + scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(1990,2025,2)) + my_theme + theme(legend.position="none") + labs(title="Domestic Urea & DAP Production in India", subtitle="Urea capacity near self-sufficient; DAP heavily import-dependent", y="Million Tonnes", x=NULL, caption="Source: Fertilizer Association of India | Analysis: Pawan") ggplotly(p_prod, tooltip="text") |> layout(hovermode="x unified") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: --- ## Section 8 · Supply Concentration Risk Summary ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} risk_data <- data.table( Fertilizer = c("Urea","DAP","Rock Phosphate","Phosphoric Acid","MOP"), Top_Supplier_1 = c("China","Saudi Arabia","Jordan","Morocco","Canada"), Top_Supplier_2 = c("Iran","China","Morocco","Jordan","Belarus"), Top2_Share_Pct = c(65, 72, 80, 85, 70), Risk_Level = c("Medium","High","High","Very High","Very High") ) risk_data |> gt() |> tab_header(title = "Fertilizer Import: Supply Concentration Risk Matrix") |> cols_label(Fertilizer="Fertilizer", Top_Supplier_1="Supplier 1", Top_Supplier_2="Supplier 2", Top2_Share_Pct="Top-2 Share %", Risk_Level="Risk Level") |> data_color(Top2_Share_Pct, colors=scales::col_numeric(c("#D5F5E3","#FADBD8"), domain=c(50,100))) |> data_color(Risk_Level, colors=scales::col_factor(c("#27AE60","#F39C12","#E74C3C","#8E44AD"), levels=c("Low","Medium","High","Very High"))) |> opt_stylize(style=3) |> tab_source_note("Source: Author's analysis based on Ministry of Commerce data") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
Fertilizer Import: Supply Concentration Risk Matrix
Fertilizer Supplier 1 Supplier 2 Top-2 Share % Risk Level
Urea China Iran 65 Medium
DAP Saudi Arabia China 72 High
Rock Phosphate Jordan Morocco 80 High
Phosphoric Acid Morocco Jordan 85 Very High
MOP Canada Belarus 70 Very High
Source: Author's analysis based on Ministry of Commerce data
``` ::: :::
